doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, Dr. Lee. My daughter has been having ear pain for the last three days, and she’s also had a fever of about 38.8°C. She’s been really uncomfortable and has missed school because of it.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the ear pain?
patient_turn: I first noticed her pulling at her ear and crying about three days ago. It seemed to start suddenly.

doctor_turn: Has the pain changed at all since it started?
patient_turn: It seems to be pretty consistent. She’s still complaining about it, and it’s been bothering her a lot.

doctor_turn: What does the pain feel like for her? Is it sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: I’m not exactly sure how she would describe it, but she seems really uncomfortable and cries when she touches it. It seems pretty intense for her.

doctor_turn: Has the pain changed at all since it first started?
patient_turn: No, it seems to be about the same since it started. She’s still in pain and hasn’t really had any relief.

doctor_turn: Has anything made the ear pain feel better or worse for her?
patient_turn: I haven’t noticed anything that makes it better or worse. She just seems to be in pain all the time, and nothing I’ve tried has helped.

doctor_turn: Can you tell me where exactly the pain is located in her ear?
patient_turn: Um, I think it’s kind of like, in the right ear, but I’m not totally sure, maybe a bit deeper inside too? It’s really hard to say exactly.

doctor_turn: Has she had any discharge from her ear?
patient_turn: No, there hasn’t been any discharge from her ear at all. It’s just the pain and fever.

doctor_turn: Has she had a cough or runny nose at any point?
patient_turn: No, she hasn’t had a cough or runny nose at all. Just the ear pain and fever.

doctor_turn: Has she been feeling unusually tired or missed school recently?
patient_turn: Yes, she’s been really tired and has missed school for the last three days because of the pain and fever.

doctor_turn: Has she had any changes in her sleep or appetite during this time?
patient_turn: No, she hasn’t had any changes in her sleep or appetite. She’s just been tired and in pain, but she’s still eating and sleeping normally.

doctor_turn: Has she had any ear infections in the past?
patient_turn: Yes, she has a history of recurrent ear infections since she was two years old. The last one was when she was seven, and she was treated with antibiotics then.

doctor_turn: When was her last ear infection before this episode?
patient_turn: Her last ear infection was when she was seven years old.

doctor_turn: Was she treated with antibiotics for her last ear infection at age seven?
patient_turn: Yes, she was treated with antibiotics for her last ear infection at age seven.

doctor_turn: Has she seen an ENT specialist for her ear problems before?
patient_turn: Yes, she saw an ENT specialist when she was four years old, but she hasn’t needed ear tubes or anything like that since then.

doctor_turn: Does she have any chronic medical conditions?
patient_turn: No, she doesn’t have any chronic medical conditions. She just takes multivitamins.

doctor_turn: What specific multivitamins does she take?
patient_turn: I don’t know. I just know she takes multivitamins, but I’m not sure which specific ones.

doctor_turn: Does she have any known allergies to medications, foods, or anything else?
patient_turn: No, she doesn’t have any known allergies.

doctor_turn: Was her birth a normal vaginal delivery without any complications?
patient_turn: Yes, her birth was a normal vaginal delivery without any complications.

doctor_turn: Have her developmental milestones been normal for her age?
patient_turn: Yes, her developmental milestones have been normal for her age.

doctor_turn: Is there any family history of ear problems or infections?
patient_turn: No, there’s no family history of ear problems or infections.

doctor_turn: Who do you live with at home?
patient_turn: She lives at home with me, her father, and her younger sibling.

doctor_turn: Has she traveled recently or been around anyone who is sick?
patient_turn: No, she hasn’t traveled recently, and she hasn’t been around anyone who is sick.
